task_description: Set up a kiosk-style restricted user account named TEST and assign it to run the Alarms & Clock app.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows the Windows taskbar at the bottom-left. A semitransparent red X is centered on the yellow File Explorer taskbar icon, positioned between the white Windows Start button and the blue-green Microsoft Edge icon. In the full-screen view, the desktop is the Windows 10 default blue wallpaper with several desktop shortcuts on the left and no open windows; this confirms the target is a taskbar UI control, not a desktop object.
caption.think: The user likely wants to open File Explorer from the taskbar.
caption.action: Click the yellow File Explorer icon on the taskbar.
caption.expectation: File Explorer should open, bringing up a folder/navigation window and making it the active window.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows the Windows search panel open in the lower-left corner. At the bottom, the white search entry field is active and currently contains the lowercase text "co", with the text cursor at the end, indicating more characters are being appended. Above it, the results list highlights "控制面板" with its blue Control Panel icon, and the right preview pane also shows "控制面板" with an "打开" option. In the full-screen view, this search panel is over the Windows 10 desktop with the default blue wallpaper and desktop shortcuts along the left side.
caption.think: The user is continuing to type into the active Windows search box to refine the search toward Control Panel by extending the existing "co" text to "CON".
caption.action: Type "CON" into the active Windows search field, replacing the current partial query.
caption.expectation: The search box should display "CON", and the search results should refresh, likely keeping or refining results related to Control Panel.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The crop shows the Windows Search panel open in the lower-left of the desktop. The search box at the bottom contains "CON". In the left results list, "控制面板" with the blue Control Panel icon is selected under Best Match, and a vertical scrollbar is visible in that results pane. The right preview pane shows the larger Control Panel icon, the label "控制面板", and an "打开" option, with a Recent section below. The full screen confirms this is on the Windows 10 desktop with the default blue wallpaper and desktop shortcuts on the left, and no other app window is active.
caption.think: The user is reviewing search results for Control Panel and scrolling upward within the search results area, likely to inspect items higher in the list while keeping Control Panel in focus.
caption.action: Scroll up in the Windows Search results panel.
caption.expectation: The results list in the search panel should move upward slightly, revealing earlier items while the search box remains "CON" and the search panel stays open.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows the Windows Search panel’s results area. A semitransparent red X is centered on a dark blank strip to the right of the results list and above the highlighted result, not on any text, icon, button, or menu item. Nearby, the top category tabs read “应用”, “文档”, “网页”, and “更多”. In the full-screen view, the Search panel is open over the Windows 10 desktop. The search box contains “CON”, the left results list has “控制面板” highlighted under Best Match, and the right preview pane shows the Control Panel icon with the “打开” option. This indicates the click is on a blank region inside the Search panel, not on a UI control.
caption.think: The user likely intends to keep focus within the search interface or dismiss any hover state without changing the current selection. Since the click lands on empty space, it should not activate a new command.
caption.action: Click the blank dark area inside the Windows Search panel above and to the right of the highlighted results item.
caption.expectation: The Search panel should remain open with “控制面板” still selected; no menu or dialog should open, and there should be little to no visible change aside from possible focus settling on the panel.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on the highlighted Control Panel category tile labeled “用户账户” with the small two-person user icon. Beneath that label is the smaller link text “更改账户类型” with a blue-and-yellow shield icon. This is a clickable category object in the main content area, not a blank region or toolbar control. In the full-screen view, the Control Panel window is open in Category view on the Windows desktop, and the “用户账户” category tile on the right side is already highlighted.
caption.think: The user likely wants to open the User Accounts section of Control Panel to access account-related settings.
caption.action: Click the “用户账户” category tile with the two-person icon in the Control Panel window.
caption.expectation: The Control Panel should navigate into the User Accounts section, opening a page with account-related settings and options.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on the blue link labeled “删除用户账户,” preceded by a blue-and-yellow shield icon. This is a clickable text link inside the highlighted “用户账户” section, so the target is a UI control rather than a blank area. In the full-screen view, a File Explorer-style Control Panel window is open to “控制面板 > 用户账户,” with the main pane showing account-related options including “更改账户类型” and “删除用户账户.”
caption.think: The user likely wants to open the account removal workflow from the User Accounts section in Control Panel.
caption.action: Click the “删除用户账户” link with the blue-and-yellow shield icon in the User Accounts pane.
caption.expectation: The window should navigate to a delete-account related page or prompt, opening the account deletion options for the selected user account.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on the blue underlined text link “在电脑设置中添加新用户.” This is a clickable text control, not a blank area. In the full-screen view, the Control Panel window is on the “管理帐户” page under “控制面板 > 用户帐户 > 用户帐户 > 管理帐户,” showing one account tile named “Administrator.” The blue link sits below the account list in the main content area.
caption.think: After opening the account management page, the user likely wants to add a new user through PC Settings rather than delete the existing Administrator account directly.
caption.action: Click the blue underlined link “在电脑设置中添加新用户.”
caption.expectation: A user-adding workflow should open, likely redirecting to Windows Settings for adding a new user account.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on the gray clickable row labeled “分配的访问权限,” with a small monitor/computer icon on the left and descriptive text underneath beginning “将此设备设置为展台...”. This is a UI control in the Settings window’s content area, not a blank region. In the full-screen view, the Windows “设置” app is open to “其他用户,” over the Control Panel “管理帐户” window in the background. The clicked row appears under the “设置展台” section.
caption.think: The user likely wants to open the Assigned Access / kiosk setup option from the Other Users settings page to configure this device as a kiosk-style station.
caption.action: Click the “分配的访问权限” row with the monitor icon under the “设置展台” section.
caption.expectation: The Settings window should open the Assigned Access configuration page or expand into a setup screen for kiosk access.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on a gray rectangular button labeled “开始.” Above it is explanatory text beginning “为此，你需要创建或选择一个本地帐户，然后选择该帐户可使用的唯一应用(已分配的访问权限).” This is clearly a clickable button control in the Settings content area. In the full-screen view, the Windows “设置” window is open to “设置展台,” with the “开始” button near the upper-middle of the page and related blue links such as “配置电源和睡眠设置” below. A Control Panel “管理帐户” window remains open behind it.
caption.think: The user likely wants to begin the kiosk/assigned-access setup wizard from the Set up a kiosk page.
caption.action: Click the gray “开始” button in the “设置展台” page.
caption.expectation: The Settings window should advance into the kiosk setup flow, likely prompting to create or choose a local account and select the app for assigned access.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a Windows Settings dialog titled “创建帐户” with explanatory text below it and a labeled text field “添加名称.” The insertion field is active with a blue outline, and the text “TE” is currently visible inside, indicating a name is being entered into the account-name box. At the bottom are disabled-looking buttons labeled “下一页” and “取消.” In the full-screen view, this dialog is centered over the Windows desktop, with a Control Panel “管理帐户” window open behind it. The typing is occurring in the kiosk/account creation flow opened from Settings, and the entered text appears to be building the new account name, appending toward “TEST.”
caption.think: The user is naming the new local account for the kiosk/assigned access setup. Since “TE” is already present and the action is Type: TEST, the intent is to enter the full account name “TEST” into the active name field.
caption.action: Type “TEST” into the active “添加名称” text box, replacing the current partial entry so the field reads “TEST.”
caption.expectation: The name field should display “TEST,” and the “下一页” button may become available so the account creation process can continue.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on the gray rectangular button labeled “下一页.” This is a UI control button at the bottom of the account-creation dialog, not a blank region. In the full-screen view, a Windows Settings dialog titled “创建帐户” is open in front of the desktop and a Control Panel “管理帐户” window. The text field labeled “添加名称” contains “TEST,” and the bottom row shows “下一页” on the left and “取消” on the right.
caption.think: The user has finished entering the new account name and now wants to proceed to the next step of the kiosk/account creation wizard.
caption.expectation: The button should activate and the dialog should advance to the next setup screen, moving forward in the account creation process.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in view shows a Windows Settings dialog titled “选择展台应用” with a scrollable list of apps. The currently visible entries include “Xbox,” “Xbox Game Bar,” “地图,” “电影和电视,” “反馈中心,” and “画图 3D,” and a vertical scrollbar is visible on the right side of this list, indicating more items below. There is no red X, which matches a scroll action. In the full-screen context, this app-selection dialog is centered over the desktop and in front of a Control Panel “管理帐户” window, continuing the kiosk setup flow after creating the account.
caption.think: The user is browsing downward through the kiosk app list to find a different app that is not currently visible on screen.
caption.action: Scroll down within the app list in the “选择展台应用” dialog.
caption.expectation: The list should move downward, revealing more applications further below while the current top items move upward out of view.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on the app list row labeled “闹钟和时钟,” with the publisher text “Microsoft Corporation” beneath it and a blue square icon containing a white alarm clock. This is a selectable list item UI control, not a blank area. In the full-screen context, the Windows Settings dialog titled “选择展台应用” is open over a Control Panel window, showing a scrollable list of apps; the “闹钟和时钟” row is currently highlighted among nearby entries such as “录音机,” “你的手机,” “人脉,” and “日历.”
caption.think: The user is choosing the Clock/Alarms app as the kiosk app from the available application list.
caption.action: Click the “闹钟和时钟” app entry in the list.
caption.expectation: That app row should become selected and the dialog should respond by confirming the choice, likely advancing the assigned-access setup or enabling the next step.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on the gray rectangular button labeled “下一页.” Above it in the list is the app entry “日历” with a blue square icon showing a white calendar and the publisher text “Microsoft Corporation.” This target is a UI control button, not a blank region. In the full-screen context, a Windows Settings dialog titled “选择展台应用” is open over Control Panel. The app list includes entries such as “录音机,” “闹钟和时钟,” “你的手机,” “人脉,” and “日历,” and “闹钟和时钟” is highlighted, indicating it has been selected.
caption.think: The user has selected the kiosk app and now wants to proceed to the next step of the assigned access setup by confirming the choice.
caption.expectation: The button should activate, confirming the selected app and advancing the setup dialog to the next screen.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on the gray rectangular button labeled “关闭.” This is a UI control button at the bottom of a dialog, not a blank region. In the full-screen context, a Windows Settings window is in front of Control Panel, showing a completion page titled “完成!” with explanatory text about kiosk mode and two bottom buttons: “关闭” on the left and a disabled-looking “取消” on the right.
caption.think: The assigned access setup appears complete, and the user now wants to dismiss the completion dialog and finish the workflow.
caption.expectation: The completion dialog should close immediately, ending this setup window and returning focus to the underlying window or desktop.
